meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Israel attacks southern Lebanon and near Syrian border despite ‘ceasefire’
items.summary: People in southern Lebanon are living under "psychological terror" from Israeli air attacks and displacement orders.

items.title: Mexico beat Ghana as World Cup cohosts excel despite FIFA sanctions
items.summary: Parts of the Puebla stadium for high-profile warm-up for 2026 edition were closed to spectators due to FIFA sanctions.

items.title: Missiles to munitions: Does the US risk running out of key weapons?
items.summary: The US projects confidence in weapons stockpiles, but analysts say dwindling munitions are shaping its war decisions.

items.title: As Salah plays final Liverpool match, his legacy goes far beyond the pitch
items.summary: Mohamed Salah's status as one of Liverpool's greatest players of all time transcends his achievements in the sport.

items.title: Failing chemical tank forces thousands to evacuate in Southern California
items.summary: Firefighters were able to reduce the tank’s temperature, buying crews more time to stabilise the situation.

items.title: North Korean women’s team win Asian Champions League title in South Korea
items.summary: Naegohyang Women's FC beat Tokyo Verdy Beleza 1-0 amid rare visit to South Korea to play in tournament.

items.title: Iran weighs US peace proposal despite ‘deep and significant’ disagreements
items.summary: A visit by Pakistan’s army chief to Tehran is seen as a sign of significant progress in negotiations.

items.title: Not just the US: India to Brazil, 51 nations armed Israel amid Gaza war
items.summary: Despite ICJ genocide warnings, 51 nations kept arming Israel, even after pledging to halt support.

items.title: Gas explosion at Chinese coal mine kills at least 90
items.summary: President Xi Jinping has called on authorities nationwide to learn from the incident.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Which star players will miss the tournament?
items.summary: Al Jazeera takes a look at the high-profile players who will play no part in the tournament in North America.

items.title: Iran war day 85: Tehran says major gaps remain in US talks
items.summary: Pakistan army chief Asim Munir arrives in Tehran as mediation efforts to secure a US-Iran peace deal intensify.

items.title: Israeli forces attack central Gaza despite ceasefire agreement
items.summary: Video shows the moment Israeli forces struck Nuseirat and Bureij refugee camps in central Gaza, injuring dozens.

items.title: Gilgeous-Alexander leads Thunder past Spurs for 2-1 NBA playoff lead
items.summary: Reigning NBA champions see four members of their bench finish in double figures to set up the precious road win.

items.title: Burn-like rashes and hunger: Gaza’s children face skin disease crisis
items.summary: Overcrowded camps in Gaza see a surge in skin diseases. Children are most affected as healthcare services collapse.

items.title: Trump releases new batch of previously classified UFO files
items.summary: The Trump administration has released a second batch of previously classified files on UFO sightings.

items.title: Over 1.6 million pilgrims arrive in Saudi Arabia for hajj pilgrimage
items.summary: More than 1.6 million Muslim pilgrims have arrived in Saudi Arabia for the annual Hajj pilgrimage

items.title: Are India and Pakistan quietly preparing to restart dialogue?
items.summary: India and Pakistan remain publicly entrenched, even as unofficial voices push for renewed dialogue and restraint.

items.title: Why are anti-migrant attacks increasing in South Africa?
items.summary: Rising xenophobia in South Africa leaves migrants facing harassment, job losses and community violence.

items.title: Some fear US-Philippines war games are drawing conflict closer to home
items.summary: Critics of US, Philippine military cooperation say closely aligning with Washington makes Manila a possible target.

items.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as Trump’s top US intelligence official
items.summary: Tulsi Gabbard has resigned as US Director of National Intelligence, citing her husband’s cancer diagnosis.

items.title: Ebola outbreak disrupts life along the DRC-Uganda border
items.summary: With almost 750 suspected Ebola cases in the DRC, health measures intensified along the DRC–Uganda border

items.title: Iran war live: Tehran says diplomacy continues but no deal yet with US
items.summary: Israeli attacks on Lebanon have killed at least 3,111 people since March 2, with 9,432 injured: Health Ministry.

items.title: Israeli blockade traps would-be Hajj pilgrim in Gaza
items.summary: For years, 64-year-old Najia Abu Lehia dreamed of performing Hajj with her husband.

items.title: Premier League finale: Arsenal, relegation, Champions League, Salah and Pep
items.summary: Arsenal may have won the title, but the Premier League story is far from over with European spots and survival in play.

items.title: ‘Everest Man’ breaks his own record after historic 32nd climb
items.summary: Legendary mountaineer Kami Rita Sherpa just broke his own record after climbing Mount Everest for the 32nd time.
